creator: p5B2EA84B3@t-online.de
text:
Context from the docs:


time.CLOCK_UPTIME

    Time whose absolute value is the time the system has been running and not suspended, providing accurate uptime measurement, both absolute and interval.

    Availability: FreeBSD, OpenBSD 5.5 or later.

    New in version 3.7.

How to reproduce:

$ python3.9
Python 3.9.13 (main, Jul  5 2022, 15:17:58) 
[Clang 13.0.0 (git@github.com:llvm/llvm-project.git llvmorg-13.0.0-0-gd7b669b3a on freebsd13
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import time
>>> time.CLOCK
...BOOTTIME            ...PROF                ...UPTIME              ...gettime_ns          
...MONOTONIC           ...REALTIME            ...getres              ...settime             
...PROCESS_CPUTIME_ID  ...THREAD_CPUTIME_ID   ...gettime             ...settime_ns          
>>> time.CLOCK_UPTIME
5
>>> 
----------
Note: This is also the case on python3.10
